items.headline: Wembanyama, Gilgeous-Alexander, Jokic named MVP finalists
items.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Nikola Jokic and Victor Wembanyama were named finalists for this season's Most Valuable Player award.

items.headline: Mason Miller closes combined 2-hitter to extend his scoreless streak as Padres edge Angels 2-1
items.description: — Xander Bogaerts and Bryce Johnson delivered two-out RBI as the San Diego Padres defeated the Los Angeles Angels 2-1 on Sunday.

items.headline: Dillon Dingler hits 3-run HR; Tigers beat struggling Crochet and Red Sox 6-2
items.description: — Dillon Dingler hit a three-run homer during Detroit’s four-run fifth inning against Boston’s struggling ace Garrett Crochet, leading the Tigers to a 6-2 victory over the Red Sox on Sunday at chilly and rainy Fenway Park.

items.headline: Cubs hand Mets their 11th straight loss by rallying for a 2-1 win in 10 innings
items.description: — Nico Hoerner hit a sacrifice fly off Craig Kimbrel in the 10th inning, and the Chicago Cubs sent the New York Mets to their 11th consecutive loss by rallying for a 2-1 victory Sunday.

items.headline: Shadasia Green still in ICU, suffered brain bleed in loss
items.description: Former unified women's super middleweight champion Shadasia Green, who lost to Lani Daniels on Friday, took to social media Sunday to provide an update on her status, stating that she was still in the ICU and suffered a brain bleed in the fight.

items.headline: Stocks: Wahs are up, Dogs dive, Storm stay down
items.description: Round 7 concluded with an upset of epic proportions as the Eels completely embarrassed the Bulldogs in what can only be described as a typical result for this infuriating season.

items.headline: WWE WrestleMania 42 Night 2: Live match results and analysis
items.description: ESPN breaks down all the action from Night 2 of WrestleMania 42 in Las Vegas.

items.headline: Judge and Rice hit early homers off Ragans as Yankees beat skidding Royals 7-0 for 3-game sweep
items.description: — Aaron Judge hit a two-run homer in the first inning, Ben Rice went deep for the fourth straight game in the second and the New York Yankees beat the reeling Kansas City Royals 7-0 to complete a three-game sweep on Sunday following a long rain delay.

items.headline: Woo goes 7 strong innings as the Mariners power past the Rangers 5-2
items.description: — Bryan Woo pitched seven strong innings, Rob Refsnyder homered on the first pitch of the bottom of the first and the Seattle Mariners topped the Texas Rangers 5-2 on Sunday.

items.headline: Munetaka Murakami homers and White Sox go deep 4 times to back Noah Schultz in 7-4 win over A's
items.description: — Rookie Munetaka Murakami and Miguel Vargas hit two of Chicago's four home runs off Jeffrey Springs, and rookie Noah Schultz allowed one hit over five innings for his first major league win as the Chicago White Sox beat the Athletics 7-4 on Sunday.

items.headline: Guardians' Austin Hedges gets engaged on field following game
items.description: Austin Hedges scored the first run of the game for the Guardians but had a bigger moment after the final out.

items.headline: Fitzpatrick beats Scheffler in playoff at RBC Heritage
items.description: Matt Fitzpatrick delivered another playoff win in the RBC Heritage, fending off Scottie Scheffler for his second title at the tournament.

items.headline: Blue Jays ride an 8-run first inning to a 10-4 win over the Diamondbacks, snapping 4-game skid
items.description: — Kazuma Okamoto hit a solo homer and had three RBI, Nathan Lukes added a three-run double and the Toronto Blue Jays used an eight-run first inning to cruise past the Arizona Diamondbacks for a 10-4 victory Sunday that prevented a three-game sweep.

items.headline: Women in black confound Australia sevens stars again
items.description: Australia's rugby sevens women have suffered yet another final let-down against their great New Zealand rivals, missing out on the marquee Hong Kong Sevens crown with a 19-14 defeat.

items.headline: Moniak and Karros homer as the Rockies beat the Dodgers 9-6
items.description: — Mickey Moniak hit a go-ahead two-run homer in the seventh, and the Colorado Rockies held on to beat Los Angeles 9-6 on Sunday and give the Dodgers’ their first losing streak of the season.
